rulings.Case Title: ADOLFO ORTEGA VS. PLANT CONSTRUCTION COMPANY, L.P. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Defts' Employees Chris Mosher, Jeff Moder, And Mark Godfey; Request For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, December 20, 2018, Line 1, PLAINTIFF ADOLFO ORTEGA's Motion To Compel Defendant's Chris Mosher, Jeff Moder, And Mark Godfey; Request For Monetary Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge Bruce Highman, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Granted. Sanctions are awarded. Hearing date required as to date(s) of depos and amount of sanctions. The court retains jurisdiction to insure that the depos proceed appropriately on the scheduled date(s). Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to bruce.highman@highmanlaw.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested.=(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: ADOLFO ORTEGA VS. PLANT CONSTRUCTION COMPANY, L.P. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Leave To Amend
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, December 20, 2018, Line 2, PLAINTIFF ADOLFO ORTEGA's Motion To Leave To Amend Plaintiff Adolfo Ortega's motion for leave to amend the complaint to seek punitive damages is granted on the following two conditions: 1) the trial is continued to May 27, 2019 at 9:30am in department 206 and 2) discovery deadlines are triggered off the new trial date only for the new allegations. While California has a liberal policy of allowing amendments to pleadings, that policy does not apply where an opposing party would suffer legal prejudice as a result of an allowing an amended pleading. The two conditions are necessary to eliminate legal prejudice to defendants who, absent the conditions, would be deprived of the ability to conduct discovery and to engage in motion practice regarding the new allegations that significantly expand their exposure. If Mr. Ortega does not accept the two conditions by his counsel's preparation of a proposed order stating acceptance of those conditions or orally accepting the conditions at the hearing, the motion is denied. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Mr. Ortega is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and states whether Mr. Ortega accepts or rejects the two conditions and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HK)

rulings.Case Title: JASON TRAVIS VS. HARRIETT M WILSON
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion Of Defendant Harriet M. Wilson To Compel Further Responses To Special Interrogatories, Set Two; Request For Order Awarding Sanctions Against Plaintiff And His Attorney In The Sum Of $1,320.00; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Thereof
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, December 20, 2018, Line 2, DEFENDANT HARRIETT WILSON, THE HARRIETT M. WILSON TRUST's Motion Of Defendant Harriet M. Wilson To Compel Further Responses To Special Interrogatories, Set Two; Request For Order Awarding Sanctions Against Plaintiff And His Attorney In The Sum Of $1,320.00. Pro Tem Judge Bruce Highman, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Denied as to interrogatories 97, 103, 104, 112, 117, 122, 132, 133, 138, 141, 142 and 150. The information requested by these interrogatories was sufficiently requested by other interrogatories. The motion is otherwise granted. The interrogatories as to which the motion is granted are not sufficiently the same as the prior discovery requests plaintiff cites. Among other things, the court notes that: (1) the plaintiff answers deposition questions off the top of his head but has to do appropriate investigation when answering an interrogatory; and (2) one can't necessarily tell from responses to document requests which documents are being produced in response to which document request, so interrogatories asking for documents to be identified are appropriate. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to bruce.highman@highmanlaw.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested.=(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: GENERATION NAILS A SOLE PROPRIETORSHIP VS. SENTINEL INSURANCE COMPANY LIMITED, A CORPORATION
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Sentinel Insurance Company, Ltd.S Notice Of Motion And Motion For Summary Judgment; Memorandum Of Points & Authorities
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, December 20, 2018, Line 6, DEFENDANT SENTINEL INSURANCE COMPANY, LTD'S Motion For Summary Judgment Defendant Sentinel Insurance Company, Ltd.'s motion for summary judgment is granted. The undisputed evidence shows that placing Mr. Ilaoa's feet in the water basin was an inseparable part of the pedicure process and the professional services exclusion applies as a matter of law. (See 16 CCR §§ 977; 980.1-980.4.) The footbath was a professional service in the sense that it constituted an aspect of the nail salon profession and was done for financial gain. There is no ambiguity in the exclusion. The injury arose during the rendering of skin enhancement and grooming services and the footbath was not "an activity incidentally related to [plaintiffs'] everyday operations." (Hollingsworth v. Commercial Union Ins. Co. (1989) 208 Cal.App.3d 800, 808; see also Antles v. The Aetna Casualty and Surety Company (1963) 221 Cal.App.2d 438 (chiropractic patient burned by defective heat lamp not covered by CGL policy because it occurred during the rendering of professional services).) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Sentinel is required to prepare a proposed order in compliance with CCP 437c(g) which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HK)

rulings.Case Title: ROBERTO BARAJAS VS. SHI YUAN KUANG
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike Portions Of Plaintiffs Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, December 20, 2018, Line 7, DEFENDANTs SHI KUANG and ALLISON WONG's Motion To Strike Portions Of Plaintiffs Complaint Defendants Shi Yuan Kuang and Allison Wong's motion to strike the punitive damages allegations and prayer in the complaint filed by plaintiff Roberto Barajas is denied. While perhaps not artfully alleged, liberally construed, the allegation that Mr. Barajas was told that Ms. Kuang consumed alcohol prior to the collision adequately alleges that Ms. Kuang acted despicably and in willful disregard of the safety of those she might encounter when she was knowingly driving a vehicle in an impaired condition. rulings.Case Title: J GARCIA CARRION, SA, A CORPORATION ORGANIZED AND ET AL VS. AMERICAN ARBITRATION ASSOCIATION INC. A NEW YORK ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, December 20, 2018, Line 8, DEFENDANTs VINCENT FRIEND, FRIEND WINE MARKETING, INC., and CORDELINA WINE COMPANY's DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT Defendants Vincent Friend, Friend Wine Marketing, Inc., and Cordelina Wine Company, LLC's demurrer to all six causes of action in the second amended complaint filed by plaintiffs J Garcia Carrion, SA and CIV USA, Inc. is sustained without leave to amend as to the first, second, fourth, and fifth causes of action for breach of contract, promissory estoppel, injunctive relief, and specific performance and off calendar as moot as to the third and sixth causes of action for unfair business practices and conversion. Plaintiffs have not alleged, nor does it appear that they are able to allege, ultimate facts supporting the existence of either an agreement or a clear and unambiguous promise to transfer the FWM stock to JGC and thus their claims seeking the transfer of the stock fail as a matter of law. As a matter of law, Exhibit A to the second amended complaint does not show either an enforceable agreement or a clear and unambiguous promise to do or not do anything and any oral representations of the same ilk fail for the same reason. Plaintiffs have dismissed the third and sixth causes of action. To the extent that plaintiffs' other causes of action rest in whole or in part on allegations regarding the failure to transfer a domain name and failure to withdraw brand registrations, per plaintiffs' acknowledgment that they are no longer pursuing those allegations, those causes of action or portions of causes of action are moot. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for defendants is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HK)

rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, December 20, 2018, Line 5, PLAINTIFF JUSTICE INVESTORS, LP, PORTSMITH SQUARE, INC.'s Motion For Relief From Stay To Conduct Limited Discovery And For Evidentiary Hearing On Motion To Compel Arbitration. rulings.Case Title: JUSTICE INVESTORS, LP ET AL VS. GEOFFREY M. PALERMO ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff Justice Investors, Lp, And Portsmith Square, Inc.'S Motion For Relief From Stay To Conduct Limited Discovery And For Evidentiary Hearing On Motion To Compel Arbitration
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, December 20, 2018, Line 12, Plaintiff Justice Investors, LP, And Portsmith Square, Inc.'S Motion For Relief From Stay To Conduct Limited Discovery And For Evidentiary Hearing On Motion To Compel Arbitration Hearing required. The hearing will start no earlier than 10am. Plaintiffs Justice Investors, LP, and Portsmouth Square, Inc.'s motion for relief from stay to conduct limited discovery is granted and its request for live evidence at the hearing is deferred .Plaintiffs have shown good cause for conducting limited discovery to support their allegations that the MSA is void for fraud in the execution. (See Rosenthal v. Great Western Fin. Securities Corp. (1996) 14 Cal.4th 394, 416, 423 ("claims of fraud in the execution of the entire agreement are not arbitrable under either state or federal law. If the entire contract is void ab initio because of fraud, the parties have not agreed to arbitrate any controversy ¿ [fraud in the execution] goes to the inception or execution of the agreement, so that the promisor is deceived as to the nature of his act, and actually does not know what he is signing, or does not intend to enter into a contract at all, mutual assent is lacking, and [the contract] is void") (emphasis added)). The court has received insufficient information to rule on the request for live testimony and the discovery that will take place may inform the need for, scope and length of any live testimony. =(302/HK)

rulings.Case Title: CLAUDIA SALAZAR RUBIO VS. KEENWAWA DBA EATSA ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, December 20, 2018, Line 10, DEFENDANT KEENWAWA DBA EATSA's DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT Defendant Keenwawa, Inc.'s demurrer to the first, second and sixth causes of action in the first amended complaint filed by plaintiff Claudia Rubio is overruled as to the first and second causes of action for violation of the FEHA and sustained without leave to amend as to the sixth cause of action for violation of Labor Code 226(a). While no reported case has been cited that permits verification of a DFEH administrative complaint by someone other than the claimant or her counsel, no reported case has been cited that holds that the only persons who can verify a DFEH administrative complaint are the claimant and her counsel. As Keenwawa acknowledges, the DFEH regulations permit a non-attorney authorized representative to verify an administrative complaint and broad language regarding the liberal construction and remedial nature of the FEHA in Blum v. Superior Court (2006) 14 Cal. App. 4th 418 reasonably supports allowing an authorized non-attorney (at least one working for the claimant's counsel) to verify a DFEH administrative complaint. Nothing in the first amended complaint or the judicially noticeable facts shows that Ms. Rubio did not authorize Mr. Coria to verify her administrative complaint. Nor has Keenwawa identified any persuasive reason grounded in the policies underlying the FEHA why it should be immune from FEHA liability due to the fortuity that Mr. Coria rather than an attorney in his firm verified the administrative complaint. Labor Code 226(a) does not require that a wage statement state the number of sick days an employee is entitled to receive. The language and logic of Maldonado v. Epsilon Plastics, Inc. (2018) 22 Cal. App. 5th 1308 precludes a claim for violation of Labor Code 226(a) based on the allegation that a wage statement lacks information about the amount of rest break compensation an employee claims but did not receive. Because Ms. Rubio has not identified any way she can state a viable claim for violation of Labor Code 226(a), she is not given leave to amend her sixth cause of action. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Ms. Rubio is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HK)

rulings.Case Title: SHAUN SMITH VS. RASIER-CA, LLC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Arbitration
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, December 20, 2018, Line 11, DEFENDANTs RASIER-CA, LLC, RAISER, LLC, UBER USA, LLC, UATC LLC, and UBER TECHNOLOGIES, INC's Motion To Compel Arbitration Defendants Rasier-CA, LLC, Rasier, LLC, Uber Technologies, Inc., Uber USA, LLC, and UATC, LLC's motion to compel arbitration is granted and this case is staying pending the conclusion of arbitration proceedings. The delegation clause in the parties' agreement clearly and unmistakably states that the arbitrator will decide "the enforceability, revocability or validity of the Arbitration Provision or any portion of the Arbitration Provision." (December 2015 TSA, Section 15.i.). Plaintiff Shaun Smith does not substantively challenge the delegation clause. Therefore, the arbitrator must decide whether there exists an enforceable arbitration agreement that will require the parties to arbitrate the underlying merits of this labor dispute or whether Mr. Smith opted out of the arbitration agreement. (O'Connor v. Uber Technologies, Inc. (9th Cir. 2018) 904 F.3d 1087, 1094-1095). Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling adversely affects, in whole or in part, any party who is not represented by an attorney, then any other party (whether represented by an attorney or not) may submit on the tentative ruling without appearing for the hearing only if the adversely affected self-represented party has stated in writing or orally that it, he or she will not appear at the hearing. Counsel for defendants is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portions of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HK)
